jokes.text: And a plain brown package for me. I'll be taking my break now.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey delivers mail to the family members professionally
jokes.punchline: Implies his 'plain brown package' contains adult material and needs privacy
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey - perfectly delivered innuendo with his trademark dry wit. The immediate exit adds to the comedic timing.

jokes.text: And something's a foot and a half.
jokes.setup: Will says 'something's afoot' about Ashley's suspicious behavior
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself with a pun playing on 'afoot' vs actual foot measurement
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that feels a bit forced. Gets a chuckle but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: She's amassing a private fortune so she can buy out the family business... in a hostile takeover.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to explain Ashley's mysterious bank account
jokes.punchline: Applies dramatic corporate intrigue terminology to a teenage girl
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton paranoia - his business-minded perspective creates absurd conspiracy theories about his little sister.

jokes.text: A sweater and a string of pearls and you'd be Angela Lansbury.
jokes.setup: Carlton presents his elaborate conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Will compares him to the elderly mystery-solving TV character
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that perfectly captures Carlton's tendency toward elaborate theories. The Angela Lansbury reference is unexpected and visual.

jokes.text: Well, uh, that sounds like the, uh, truth.
jokes.setup: Ashley gives an obviously fake excuse about 'casual day'
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic delivery highlights how unconvincing she sounds
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: If I know, I can't say I don't know when you get busted... and Uncle Phil start rounding up the usual suspects. And I am the usual suspects.
jokes.setup: Ashley wants to tell Will her secret
jokes.punchline: Will explains his precarious position as the automatic scapegoat
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-based humor that references Will's outsider status. The 'usual suspects' twist is clever and relatable.

jokes.text: You're worse than tripping. You've fallen and you can't get up.
jokes.setup: Will calls Ashley's plan 'tripping'
jokes.punchline: Escalates with reference to famous Life Alert commercial
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works for escalation but feels somewhat dated and predictable.

jokes.text: Everything's so fake at Bel-Air Academy. The teachers, the kids, the noses.
jokes.setup: Ashley explains why she left private school - everything's fake
jokes.punchline: Adds 'the noses' implying plastic surgery among wealthy students
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered through escalation. The 'noses' tag is unexpected and rings true for wealthy prep school culture.

jokes.text: I ain't gonna dime you... but if he subpoenas me, I'm gonna be singing like Luther.
jokes.setup: Will agrees not to tell on Ashley
jokes.punchline: Uses street/legal terminology mixed with Luther Vandross reference
jokes.explanation: Great Will line mixing street smarts with pop culture. The Luther Vandross reference is unexpected and fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: It's Hack Boy, the stubby-fingered avenger.
jokes.setup: Carlton demonstrates his hacking skills
jokes.punchline: Will creates a mock superhero name highlighting Carlton's short stature
jokes.explanation: Effective physical comedy targeting Carlton's height. The superhero parody format works well.

jokes.text: Maybe she's Counterfeit Girl, the sister of Hack Boy.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggests Ashley is involved in criminal activity
jokes.punchline: Will continues the superhero parody theme
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that extends the superhero bit, but less impactful than the original.

jokes.text: Trust no one.
jokes.setup: Carlton thinks Ashley has corrupted Will
jokes.punchline: Delivers X-Files catchphrase with paranoid conviction
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits Carlton's paranoid state, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Dysfunctional, uh, single parent, uh, gang member nuns.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks Will about his favorite topic from her talk show
jokes.punchline: Will improvises an increasingly absurd and contradictory show topic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation that parodies daytime TV topics while showing Will's quick thinking under pressure.

jokes.text: Why? What have Carlton and Ashley ever done for me?
jokes.setup: Will suggests Hilary reach out to her 'brothers and sisters'
jokes.punchline: Hilary misunderstands, thinking he means her actual siblings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - completely self-absorbed and missing the point about African American viewers. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: Whoa, if it isn't Belly D. Williams.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil enters in formal wear
jokes.punchline: Will comments on Phil's weight with a play on singer Billy Dee Williams
jokes.explanation: Typical Will fat joke about Uncle Phil. The Billy Dee Williams reference adds some creativity but it's still body humor.

jokes.text: Do I hate these things. - Why agree to be on the Board of Trustees? - Well, I'm a judge and judges are supposed to be on boards. - Well, it's just that with you, it's usually a smorgasbord.
jokes.setup: Phil explains judges serve on boards
jokes.punchline: Will turns 'boards' into 'smorgasbord' referencing Phil's appetite
jokes.explanation: Another Phil weight joke, but the wordplay from 'board' to 'smorgasbord' shows some wit in the construction.

jokes.text: It's girl talk. (in female voice)
jokes.setup: Ashley needs to talk to Will privately
jokes.punchline: Will uses exaggerated feminine voice to deflect Phil's curiosity
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy bit. Gets a laugh but relies on basic gender humor.

jokes.text: When we go shopping for my cap and gown. That's assuming that you're gonna live till graduation.
jokes.setup: Ashley says she's waiting for the right time to tell her dad
jokes.punchline: Will implies Uncle Phil might kill her before graduation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates the stakes. Will's matter-of-fact delivery of the threat makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Ashley, let me sum this up for you in two words: Unh-unh.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks for Will's help
jokes.punchline: Will says 'two words' then uses a sound effect that isn't words
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. The setup promises 'two words' but delivers a dismissive sound. Shows Will's street attitude.

jokes.text: My fault? Oh, this one here gonna be good.
jokes.setup: Ashley blames Will for her situation
jokes.punchline: Will's incredulous reaction anticipating Ashley's twisted logic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's street-smart anticipation of being scapegoated.

jokes.text: It's like a drug, Will, and you got me hooked.
jokes.setup: Ashley explains how Will opened her eyes to the real world
jokes.punchline: Compares exposure to public school to drug addiction
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that escalates Ashley's justification to absurd levels. The drug comparison is unexpected.

jokes.text: Well, you better go cold turkey before you wind up a dead duck.
jokes.setup: Ashley compares her situation to drug addiction
jokes.punchline: Will continues the metaphor mixing 'cold turkey' with 'dead duck'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that continues the addiction metaphor with animal idioms, but feels somewhat forced.

jokes.text: I'm a terrible, terrible person. [SOBBING]
jokes.setup: Ashley is trying to manipulate Will into helping her
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top fake crying and self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Standard manipulation tactic played for comedy. The over-acting makes it funny rather than genuine

jokes.text: What? You smiled. I saw your reflection in the window.
jokes.setup: Ashley successfully manipulates Will with fake tears
jokes.punchline: Will catches her satisfied smile in the window reflection
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy that pays off the manipulation setup. The window reflection detail is clever and cinematic.

jokes.text: Oh, no. That was a happy smile. See?
jokes.setup: Will catches Ashley's manipulative smile
jokes.punchline: Ashley doubles down with an even more obvious fake innocent smile
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Ashley digging herself deeper. The obvious overacting makes it funnier.

jokes.text: But if I go down, I'm taking you with me.
jokes.setup: Will refuses to help after catching her manipulation
jokes.punchline: Ashley threatens to implicate Will in her deception
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's ruthless side. The threat escalates the stakes effectively.

jokes.text: There's my little bran muffin. (Will in deep voice as fake father)
jokes.setup: Will arrives disguised as Ashley's father to meet her teacher
jokes.punchline: Uses ridiculously deep voice and odd pet name
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy setup. Will's fake deep voice and 'bran muffin' pet name are both absurd and endearing.

jokes.text: Rhaul, please.
jokes.setup: Teacher asks if this is Ashley's father
jokes.punchline: Will gives himself the ridiculous fake name 'Rhaul'
jokes.explanation: The made-up name 'Rhaul' is absurd enough to be funny. Will's commitment to the character sells it.

jokes.text: How could something so smooth be called sharp?
jokes.setup: Will is introduced to Miss Sharpe
jokes.punchline: Makes a smooth pickup line playing on her name vs. her appearance
jokes.explanation: Smooth pickup line that plays on the teacher's name. Shows Will's natural charm even in disguise.

jokes.text: God bless you, Miss Sharpe. (after fake Japanese)
jokes.setup: Teacher mentions Will just returned from the Orient, Will speaks fake Japanese
jokes.punchline: Treats the Japanese sounds like a sneeze and says 'God bless you'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Will covers his inability to speak Japanese by pretending it was a sneeze. Creative and well-executed.

jokes.text: Uh, no, I'm single. I mean, um, Ashley's mother ran out on us.
jokes.setup: Teacher asks if Mrs. Banks will join them
jokes.punchline: Will awkwardly improvises that he's single and Ashley's mother abandoned them
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Will digs himself deeper with unnecessary tragic backstory.

jokes.text: We went from training wheels to training bras. They both came off too soon, didn't they kitten?
jokes.setup: Will explains raising Ashley alone
jokes.punchline: Makes inappropriate parallel between bike training and bra development
jokes.explanation: Cringey inappropriate father comment that makes everyone uncomfortable. The parallel structure works but the content is awkward.

jokes.text: You know, that runs in the family.
jokes.setup: Teacher compliments Ashley as 'a pleasure to have around'
jokes.punchline: Will suggests being pleasant is hereditary (implying he's pleasant too)
jokes.explanation: Simple self-congratulatory humor. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Denzel ain't the only brother than can act.
jokes.setup: Ashley amazed that Will's performance worked
jokes.punchline: Will compares himself to Denzel Washington's acting ability
jokes.explanation: Good confident Will moment. The Denzel comparison shows his swagger and fits his character.

jokes.text: I made that up.
jokes.setup: Ashley calls Will 'Rhaul'
jokes.punchline: Will matter-of-factly admits the name was improvised
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. The economy of just stating the obvious makes it funny.

jokes.text: I, uh, forgot to give you the number to my direct line.
jokes.setup: Will returning to the classroom
jokes.punchline: Obviously going back to hit on the teacher
jokes.explanation: Predictable return to hit on the teacher, but consistent with Will's character.

jokes.text: That is an absolutely intoxicating fragrance that you're wearing.
jokes.setup: Will returns to hit on Miss Sharpe
jokes.punchline: Uses overly flowery language to compliment her perfume
jokes.explanation: Standard pickup line that's supposed to be cringey. Works for character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: That's a fake mustache. - No, it's not. - Yes, it is. - No, it's not.
jokes.setup: Miss Sharpe discovers Will's disguise
jokes.punchline: Will ridiculously insists his obvious fake mustache is real
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation. Will's stubborn denial in the face of obvious evidence is childishly funny.

jokes.text: Will? / No, it's not.
jokes.setup: Teacher recognizing Will
jokes.punchline: Will maintaining his denial even about his identity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Will's denial becomes completely absurd, showing his commitment to the bit.

jokes.text: Look, ease up, Miss Tubman.
jokes.setup: Ashley dramatically laments their lost freedom
jokes.punchline: Will compares her to Underground Railroad conductor Harriet Tubman
jokes.explanation: Excellent historical reference that perfectly captures Ashley's dramatic overreaction to being caught. Smart and culturally specific.

jokes.text: Then I should be honored that she thinks I look like a black Alfred Hitchcock.
jokes.setup: Vivian suggests the mayor's mother meant her comment as a compliment
jokes.punchline: Phil sarcastically embraces being compared to the portly film director
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware humor from Phil about his weight. The Alfred Hitchcock comparison is unexpected and visual.

jokes.text: Good evening. (imitating Hitchcock)
jokes.setup: Phil mentions looking like Alfred Hitchcock
jokes.punchline: Will immediately does Hitchcock impression
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that extends the Hitchcock bit. Simple but effective physical comedy.

jokes.text: Will has something he wants to tell you. I ain't got nothing.
jokes.setup: Ashley tries to throw Will under the bus
jokes.punchline: Will immediately denies any involvement
jokes.explanation: Classic Will self-preservation moment. Quick denial shows his street smarts

jokes.text: Because most parents want the truth, but they can't handle the truth.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain why Ashley shouldn't tell the truth
jokes.punchline: References famous Jack Nicholson line from 'A Few Good Men'
jokes.explanation: Classic pop culture reference that fits the context well. Recognizable and quotable but not particularly original.

jokes.text: So in conclusion, I move for a change of venue. You give us a half-hour head start.
jokes.setup: Will's fake legal defense
jokes.punchline: Asking for time to run away
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's panic, mixing legal terms with escape plan.

jokes.text: So in conclusion, I move for a change of venue. You give us a half-hour head start.
jokes.setup: Will gives nonsensical legal defense
jokes.punchline: Concludes by asking for time to escape like fugitives
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's desperate legal nonsense ending with practical escape request. Shows his street smarts.

jokes.text: Tomorrow when I wake up... Ashley will be back in Bel-Air Academy and maybe Will won't even be here.
jokes.setup: Phil appears calm about Ashley's revelation
jokes.punchline: Implies he might kick Will out or worse
jokes.explanation: Great Phil threat that escalates from reasonable to ominous. His calm delivery makes the threat funnier.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa. Pull it back, baby. Pull it back. Like I'm doing. Pull it back.
jokes.setup: Ashley starts to blame Will for her situation
jokes.punchline: Will frantically gestures for her to stop talking and demonstrates backing away
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Will literally demonstrating what he wants Ashley to do. The repetition adds to the panic.

jokes.text: Oh, and people in Bel-Air don't have real concerns? - It's pronounced Porsche. - Porsche. - Porsche. - Porsche.
jokes.setup: Ashley claims Bel-Air people don't have real concerns
jokes.punchline: Hilary and Carlton immediately prove her point by arguing over luxury car pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing that immediately validates Ashley's point about Bel-Air privilege. The escalating pronunciation argument is brilliant.

jokes.text: Whoa, good point, Ash.
jokes.setup: Ashley makes valid argument about public vs private school
jokes.punchline: Will acknowledges she scored a point against Phil
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that shows Will supporting Ashley's logic against his uncle's authority.

jokes.text: San Tropez. - San Tropez.
jokes.setup: Previous pronunciation argument about Porsche
jokes.punchline: They continue with another luxury destination pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the privilege joke. Less impactful than the original but maintains the theme.

jokes.text: Inga is wearing a hand-woven dashiki from Nairobi. Her jewelry is from the Ivory Coast... and her weave is from Whoopi Goldberg's House of Hair.
jokes.setup: Hilary does African fashion show with authentic descriptions
jokes.punchline: Ends with completely made-up Whoopi Goldberg hair store
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from authentic African culture to absurd celebrity endorsement. Perfect Hilary moment mixing shallow understanding with confidence.

jokes.text: Work it, you Nubian princess, you. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Hilary attempts to connect with African American culture
jokes.punchline: Uses awkward, patronizing language that shows her disconnection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Hilary's tone-deaf attempt at cultural connection. The forced enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: African Americans.
jokes.setup: Will asks what Hilary forgot in her African American outreach
jokes.punchline: States the obvious - she forgot actual African Americans
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that cuts to the heart of Hilary's disconnect. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Oh, nice to meet you for the first time ever in life.
jokes.setup: Will meets Miss Sharpe in front of Uncle Phil after their earlier encounter
jokes.punchline: Overemphasizes that this is their first meeting
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Will's overemphasis makes him more suspicious. The repetition adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Whoo. She really something, huh? Hard to say no to her.
jokes.setup: Miss Sharpe leaves after Phil rejects her pitch
jokes.punchline: Will ironically comments on Phil saying no to her easily
jokes.explanation: Mild ironic comment that sets up Phil's parenting inflexibility. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Snob.
jokes.setup: Will builds up to calling Uncle Phil something
jokes.punchline: Simply calls him a snob after a long pause
jokes.explanation: Great timing and economy. The long pause builds tension for a simple but effective insult that captures the conflict.

jokes.text: And a one and a... Hell with that.
jokes.setup: Will starts to count off like a band leader
jokes.punchline: Abandons the musical approach and storms off
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Will's frustration. The musical setup followed by giving up is relatable.

jokes.text: Maybe that's why they need metal detectors, huh?
jokes.setup: Vivian mentions Morris High is a 'magnet school'
jokes.punchline: Phil makes pun connecting 'magnet' to metal detectors in schools
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that plays on 'magnet school' terminology, but reinforces Phil's prejudices about public schools.

jokes.text: Sometimes we work so hard to give our kids what we didn't have... sometimes we forget to give them what we did have.
jokes.setup: Discussion about public vs private school
jokes.punchline: Vivian's wise observation about parenting paradox
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical observation that cuts to the heart of the episode's theme. More profound than purely comedic but perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: What are you eating? - Food. What are you doing? - Cutting the tension.
jokes.setup: Awkward family breakfast with obvious tension
jokes.punchline: Will matter-of-factly admits he's literally trying to cut tension
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan humor that acknowledges the elephant in the room. Will's directness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: So, what's today's black topic? Amish rap stars?
jokes.setup: Will asks about Hilary's show topic
jokes.punchline: Suggests absurd combination of Amish culture and rap music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd combination that parodies Hilary's desperate demographic pandering. The cultural clash is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Thanks to my leopard skin midriff top, I hit an all-time high with male viewers.
jokes.setup: Hilary explains her new strategy
jokes.punchline: Admits to using sex appeal instead of content quality
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary moment showing her shallow approach to success. The specific costume detail makes it visual.

jokes.text: I got more men yesterday than Sally Jesse Raphael gets in a year. - How hard could that be?
jokes.setup: Hilary brags about attracting male viewers
jokes.punchline: Will implies Sally Jesse Raphael doesn't attract men at all
jokes.explanation: Good roast that deflates Hilary's bragging by suggesting her competition isn't attractive. Sharp delivery.

jokes.text: How hard could that be?
jokes.setup: Hilary brags about getting more male viewers than Sally Jesse Raphael
jokes.punchline: Will implies Sally Jesse Raphael doesn't attract many men
jokes.explanation: Simple put-down of Sally Jesse Raphael. Mean-spirited but gets a laugh
